description_long: This guard stone is a vertically oriented, cylindrical object with a robust, carved structure and a distinct red cap at the top. The body features a hollowed central section with arched cutouts, creating a lattice-like appearance, and the lower portion is solid and rounded. The surface is weathered, with visible textural irregularities and a matte finish, suggesting it is made from stone or concrete. The red cap appears to be a separate material or a painted section, contrasting with the darker, earth-toned body. The object is designed to be sturdy and immovable, likely serving as a protective barrier to prevent vehicles from encroaching onto pedestrian areas or property. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative engravings, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A cylindrical, carved guard stone with a red cap, hollowed midsection, and weathered stone surface. Its robust form and lack of ornamentation suggest a utilitarian, protective purpose.
description_view_0: Front view shows the open, arched midsection and the prominent red cap on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thickness of the stone and the depth of the hollowed section.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the symmetry of the cutouts and the solid lower base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the curvature and the transition between the red cap and the main body.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban sidewalks, at the corners of building entrances, or at the entrance to driveways and courtyards to prevent vehicle encroachment.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright and possibly anchored into a concrete or stone base for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Surface shows signs of weathering, including discoloration and smoothing on the upper edges, likely from rain and sun exposure. Lower sections may have minor chipping or abrasions from vehicle contact or foot traffic.
